findings.issue: SelectCommonProperties scan arity: adding 5 GUC columns to the SELECT (Task 4) requires updating the .Scan(...) call in GetPostgresProperties in the same task. The existing Scan at internal/stat/postgres.go:126-134 scans exactly 8 columns; adding 5 columns without updating Scan produces a scan-arity runtime error on startup. The tech-spec explicitly calls this out as a lockstep requirement and lists it as a risk with the mitigation of updating in the same task. The test postgres_test.go:TestGetPostgresProperties covers this path with a live PG. The risk is real but the mitigation (single-task lockstep + test coverage) is sound.
findings.location: Backward Compatibility; Risks → SelectCommonProperties scan-arity break; Task 4
findings.fix: No additional fix needed beyond what the spec prescribes (lockstep edit in Task 4, verified by TestGetPostgresProperties). Ensure the task explicitly forbids splitting the SELECT extension and the Scan update into separate commits.

summary: All four round-1 findings are fixed. Decision 2 + Task 2 now carry an explicit prev.Verbose != v.Verbose → continue branch that mirrors the ShowExtra pattern and skips the unconditional Reset(). Decision 9 introduces a named verboseCollectState sub-struct with a concrete guard threshold (25% of refresh interval or 500ms floor). Task 6 documents the same-goroutine sequencing of config.verbose. Task 8 calls out the print-time /1024/128 nicstat conversion parity. The 5-wave dependency order is topologically sound: foundations → aggregates/geometry → system collection → row composers → tiering, with no intra-wave file conflicts. Approved with zero critical or major findings.
